task_completion.description: Verify the title and course information are bolded, centered, and the title is visibly larger (at least 14pt).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Midterm Examination' and 'CHEM 1311 – General Chemistry I' are centered at the top of the page, in bold font. The title 'Midterm Examination' should appear larger than the surrounding text.

task_completion.description: Verify the inclusion of underscored input fields for Name, Student ID, Section, and Date.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'Name:', 'Student ID:', 'Section:', and 'Date:' followed by visible underscore lines (e.g., 'Name: _________') near the top of the document.

task_completion.description: Verify that heading styles are applied to the three exam parts.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The phrases 'Part I: Multiple Choice', 'Part II: Short Answer', and 'Part III: Calculation Problems' stand out from the normal body text (e.g., larger font, bold, or distinct heading style).

task_completion.description: Verify that the point distribution and physical constants are formatted as tables.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for grid lines or clearly aligned tabular structures containing point values (e.g., 30, 40, 100) and physical constants (e.g., 8.314, 6.022).

task_completion.description: Verify that the multiple choice questions have clearly structured options (A-D) and body text appears to be a standard readable size (at least 11pt).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Under 'Part I: Multiple Choice', the questions are followed by options clearly labeled A, B, C, and D, formatted in a structured list or aligned layout. The general body text should not appear unusually small.

task_completion.description: Verify the document is saved to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The application title bar, tab name, or a save dialog shows the filename 'chem1311_midterm.odt'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a graphical word processor to format the document.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a word processing application interface (like Calligra Words or LibreOffice Writer) with formatting toolbars, not a terminal or plain text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the formatting is applied using word processor features rather than plain text workarounds.
integrity.visual_evidence: Tables should have actual cell structures/borders, and centered text should use alignment tools (visible in the toolbar being active) rather than just typed spaces or tabs.

integrity.description: Verify the original chemistry content was preserved.
integrity.visual_evidence: The document contains actual chemistry terms (e.g., 'electron configuration', 'stoichiometry') and is not replaced by generic placeholder text.
